vibe: Dub techno for wandering Roppongi Hills at night — deep, hypnotic, minimal. The sound of neon reflecting off wet concrete at 5 PM in March.

tracks.name: Quadrant Dub I
tracks.reason: The blueprint. Moritz von Oswald and Mark Ernestus invented dub techno with this — 15 minutes of pure hypnosis. The reverb IS the instrument.

tracks.name: Vantage Isle - DC Mix I
tracks.reason: Rod Modell's Deepchord project — Detroit meets Berlin in the most beautiful way. Sounds like driving through fog at 3 AM.

tracks.name: Oblique
tracks.reason: Greek producer Konstantinos Soublis — Vibrant Forms II is a masterclass in making emptiness feel full. Every echo has a purpose.

tracks.name: Ghosts
tracks.reason: Robert Henke (also creator of Ableton Live) — this track feels like the space between buildings in Roppongi at dusk.

tracks.name: King in My Empire
tracks.reason: Basic Channel's dub reggae alias with vocalist Tikiman. Where Berlin dub techno meets Kingston — the warmest thing on this playlist.

tracks.name: Nautical Dub
tracks.reason: Harder, darker dub techno — Chain Reaction label energy. Named after an oceanic research station, sounds exactly like one.

tracks.name: Butterfly Effect
tracks.reason: Japanese dub techno legend — reclusive Saitama producer, barely any interviews. This 12-minute piece is delicate and devastating.

tracks.name: Lyot - Maurizio Mix
tracks.reason: Vainqueur (René Löwe) remixed by Maurizio (Moritz von Oswald). Two dub techno gods on one track — Chain Reaction perfection.

tracks.name: Faith in Strangers
tracks.reason: Manchester producer pushing dub techno into something more emotional. The vocal samples floating through distortion — gorgeous and unsettling.

tracks.name: Steady as a rock
tracks.reason: Scott Monteith — Montreal-via-Berlin dub techno with reggae DNA. The perfect closer, like the last train home.
